subtopics.title: Digital Transformation and Economic Formalization – GST, UPI, and More

blocks.title: Digital Transformation and Economic Formalization
blocks.subtitle: India's Digital Revolution and Tax Reforms

blocks.title: Digital India Initiative (2015)
blocks.content: The Digital India programme was launched by Prime Minister Narendra Modi in 2015. Its goal is to transform India into a digitally empowered society. Key pillars: Broadband highways, universal mobile connectivity, public internet access, e-governance, and digital literacy.

blocks.title: The UPI Revolution
blocks.content: The Unified Payments Interface (UPI) is a real-time payment system developed by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) in 2016. It enables instant money transfers between bank accounts using mobile phones. UPI has become one of the world's largest digital payment systems, processing billions of transactions monthly.

blocks.title: Goods and Services Tax (GST) – 2017
blocks.content: GST is India's biggest tax reform, implemented on 1st July 2017. It replaced a complex system of multiple taxes (excise, VAT, service tax) with a single, unified tax structure. GST is a destination-based tax with rates: 0%, 5%, 12%, 18%, 28%. It simplified compliance, reduced cascading effects, and increased tax revenue.

blocks.title: Demonetisation (2016)
blocks.content: On 8th November 2016, Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced the demonetisation of ₹500 and ₹1000 currency notes, which accounted for 86% of India's currency in circulation. The move was aimed at curbing black money, counterfeiting, and promoting digital payments.

blocks.title: Key Digital and Economic Initiatives
rows: Removal of ₹500/₹1000 notes, push for digital payments.
Unified tax system, 'One Nation, One Tax'.

blocks.title: Example: The Impact of GST
given: GST was implemented in 2017, replacing multiple taxes.
blocks.find: What has been the impact of GST on the Indian economy?
steps: Increased tax revenue due to improved compliance.
Created a common national market (One Nation, One Tax).
blocks.answer: GST has simplified taxation, increased formalisation, and boosted tax revenue.

blocks.title: Key Points
points: Digital India (2015): Digital infrastructure, e-governance.
UPI (2016): India's largest digital payments platform.
Demonetisation (2016): Removed ₹500/₹1000 notes, push for digital payments.
GST (2017): Unified tax, 'One Nation, One Tax', simplified compliance.

subtopics.title: Mega Infrastructure and Connectivity – Highways, Rail Corridors, and Rural Electrification

blocks.title: Rapid Expansion of National Highways
blocks.content: India has witnessed a rapid expansion of its national highway network under the Bharatmala Pariyojana (2017). It aims to construct 83,000 km of highways. The pace of highway construction has increased from 12 km/day (2014) to over 30 km/day.

blocks.title: High-Speed Rail Corridors
blocks.content: India's first high-speed rail corridor (bullet train) is being built between Mumbai and Ahmedabad. The project is being developed with Japanese technology (Shinkansen). It will connect India's financial capital with a major economic hub.

blocks.title: Rural Electrification
blocks.content: The Saubhagya Scheme (2017) was launched to achieve universal household electrification. By 2019, all 2.8 crore un-electrified households were connected to the grid. India achieved 100% village electrification and household electrification.

blocks.title: Railway Modernisation
blocks.content: Indian Railways has seen significant modernisation: Vande Bharat Express (semi-high-speed trains), electrification of railway lines (100% by 2024), and the development of Dedicated Freight Corridors (DFCs) to boost freight transport.

blocks.title: Example: India's 100% Rural Electrification
given: India achieved 100% household electrification under the Saubhagya Scheme.
blocks.find: What was the significance of this achievement?
steps: Improved quality of life in rural areas.
Enabled digital connectivity and access to services.
Reduced reliance on kerosene and other fossil fuels.
blocks.answer: Rural electrification improved living standards and boosted the economy.

blocks.title: Trap: Vande Bharat Express is a bullet train.
blocks.wrong: Vande Bharat Express is a bullet train.
blocks.correct: Vande Bharat Express is a semi-high-speed train (130-160 km/h). The Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train will be the first high-speed rail.
blocks.tip: Vande Bharat = Semi-high speed; Bullet train = Mumbai-Ahmedabad.

blocks.title: Key Points
points: Bharatmala: 83,000 km highways, improved connectivity.
Mumbai-Ahmedabad Bullet Train: India's first high-speed rail.
Saubhagya: 100% household electrification (2017-2019).
Vande Bharat: India's first semi-high-speed train (2019).
Infrastructure development has boosted economic growth and connectivity.

subtopics.title: Advanced Space and Defense Horizons – Chandrayaan, Mangalyaan, Make in India

blocks.title: Advanced Space and Defense Horizons
blocks.subtitle: India's Giant Leaps in Space and Defense

blocks.title: ISRO's Space Achievements
blocks.content: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has achieved remarkable success in recent years. Key missions include the Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan, 2014) – India's first interplanetary mission, and Chandrayaan-2 (2019) – India's second lunar mission with an orbiter, lander (Vikram), and rover (Pragyan).

blocks.title: Chandrayaan-3 (2023) – A Historic Success
blocks.content: Chandrayaan-3 was launched on 14th July 2023 and successfully landed near the lunar south pole on 23rd August 2023. India became the first country to land near the lunar south pole and the fourth country (after USA, Russia, China) to achieve a soft landing on the Moon. The Vikram lander and Pragyan rover conducted experiments on the lunar surface.

blocks.title: Aditya-L1 – India's Solar Mission (2023)
blocks.content: Aditya-L1 is India's first space-based observatory to study the Sun. It was launched on 2nd September 2023. It will study solar dynamics, coronal mass ejections, and solar wind from the Lagrange Point 1 (L1) orbit.

blocks.title: Defense Self-Reliance – Make in India in Defense
blocks.content: The Indian government has promoted defense manufacturing under the 'Make in India' initiative. Key achievements: indigenous aircraft carrier INS Vikrant (commissioned in 2022), Tejas (indigenous fighter aircraft), Arjun (main battle tank), and Akash (surface-to-air missile system). India has also reduced dependence on foreign arms imports.

blocks.title: Light Combat Aircraft (LCA) Tejas
blocks.content: Tejas is India's indigenous light combat aircraft developed by the Aeronautical Development Agency (ADA) and Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL). It is a single-engine, multi-role fighter aircraft. It was inducted into the Indian Air Force in 2015. Tejas is a symbol of India's self-reliance in defense technology.

blocks.title: Major Space and Defense Achievements
rows: India's first interplanetary mission, reached Mars on first attempt.
India's second lunar mission; orbiter successful, lander lost contact.
First soft landing near lunar south pole, historic success.
India's first solar mission, studying the Sun.

blocks.title: Example: Chandrayaan-3 – A Historic Mission
given: Chandrayaan-3 successfully landed near the lunar south pole in 2023.
blocks.find: What was the significance of this mission?
steps: India became the first country to land near the lunar south pole.
India became the fourth country to achieve a soft landing on the Moon.
The mission conducted experiments on the lunar surface (water ice, minerals).
It demonstrated India's advanced space technology and cost-effectiveness.
blocks.answer: Chandrayaan-3 marked a historic achievement in lunar exploration.

blocks.title: Trap: Chandrayaan-2 was a complete success.
blocks.correct: Chandrayaan-2's orbiter was successful, but the lander (Vikram) lost contact during the descent.
blocks.tip: Chandrayaan-2: Orbiter = Success; Lander/Rover = Partial (lost contact).

blocks.title: Key Points
points: Mangalyaan (2014): India's first Mars mission.
Chandrayaan-3 (2023): First soft landing near lunar south pole.
Aditya-L1 (2023): India's first solar mission.
INS Vikrant (2022): India's first indigenous aircraft carrier.
Tejas: India's indigenous light combat aircraft.
Make in India: Strengthened defense self-reliance.

blocks.title: Exam Strategy
blocks.content: Focus on GST and UPI (economic reforms), Digital India, Chandrayaan missions, defense initiatives, and infrastructure projects. Memorize key years and terms.

blocks.question: GST was implemented in India in which year?
blocks.explanation: GST was implemented on 1st July 2017. It replaced multiple indirect taxes with a unified tax system.

blocks.question: Which Indian space mission successfully landed near the lunar south pole?
blocks.explanation: Chandrayaan-3 successfully landed near the lunar south pole on 23rd August 2023.

blocks.question: What does UPI stand for?
blocks.explanation: UPI stands for Unified Payments Interface, a real-time payment system developed by NPCI in 2016.

blocks.question: Which initiative was launched to achieve 100% household electrification?
blocks.explanation: The Saubhagya Scheme (2017) was launched to achieve universal household electrification, completed by 2019.

blocks.question: The Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train project is being developed with which country's technology?
blocks.explanation: The Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train is being developed with Japanese technology (Shinkansen).

blocks.question: Which is India's first indigenous aircraft carrier?
blocks.explanation: INS Vikrant (2022) is India's first indigenous aircraft carrier.

blocks.question: The Tejas is an indigenous:
blocks.explanation: Tejas is India's indigenous light combat aircraft (LCA), developed by ADA and HAL.

blocks.question: Which year was the Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) launched?
blocks.explanation: The Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) was launched in November 2013 and reached Mars in 2014.

blocks.question: Which scheme aims to construct 83,000 km of national highways?
blocks.explanation: Bharatmala Pariyojana (2017) aims to construct 83,000 km of national highways.

blocks.question: What was the primary objective of demonetisation in 2016?
options: To curb black money and counterfeiting
blocks.explanation: Demonetisation in 2016 was aimed at curbing black money, counterfeiting, and promoting digital payments.

blocks.title: Post-Test Review
blocks.content: Review key initiatives: GST (2017), UPI (2016), Digital India (2015), Chandrayaan-3 (2023), and defense achievements (INS Vikrant, Tejas). Memorize important years and their significance.

blocks.title: Final Revision – Contemporary India
points: Digital India (2015): Digital infrastructure, e-governance.
UPI (2016): India's largest digital payment system.
GST (2017): 'One Nation, One Tax', unified tax system.
Chandrayaan-3 (2023): First soft landing near lunar south pole.
Aditya-L1 (2023): India's first solar mission.
INS Vikrant (2022): First indigenous aircraft carrier.
Tejas: India's indigenous light combat aircraft.
Bharatmala (2017): 83,000 km highways.
Saubhagya (2017): 100% household electrification.
Make in India: Strengthened defense manufacturing and self-reliance.
